items.acceptance_criteria: An MCP client calling tools/list against a gitcode-mcp --mcp server with a writable cache sees repo_status, sync_live, index_repo, auth_status, and doctor tool names. A mocked MCP test initializes an empty writable cache, calls repo_status (returns nothing bound), calls sync_live --issues (returns sync event with fresh count), calls index_repo (proves it invokes Service.Index by observing index outcome, not stale-index diagnostic), and then calls MCP search_sources / list_sources (returns the synced records). A separate test adds a new lifecycle tool and proves the tool registry returns the correct handler by name, not by positional coincidence. A test calls a known write tool name via MCP tools/call and receives the structured unsupported_capability result without an outbound HTTP call or credential lookup.
items.actor_trigger: MCP client calls tools/list against gitcode-mcp --mcp server with writable cache
items.expected_outcome: tools/list returns repo_status, sync_live, index_repo, auth_status, doctor; calling repo_status on empty cache returns nothing bound; calling sync_live --issues returns sync event with fresh count; calling index_repo invokes Service.Index; calling create_issue returns unsupported_capability without credential lookup or HTTP call; tool registry resolves handlers by name, not positional index
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: MCP lifecycle/control-plane tool surface with repo_status, sync_live, index_repo, auth_status, doctor; unsupported_capability for writes; name-based tool registry
items.target_surface: MCP server tools/list and tools/call over stdio transport

items.acceptance_criteria: A mocked MCP test starts the server with a cache path pointing to a read-only directory; tools/list still returns at least one tool and the tool result or server capability block includes cache_path_unwritable diagnostic. A test with a schema-incompatible cache returns schema_incompatible diagnostic. A test with a writer-locked cache returns cache_lock_contention diagnostic, not a vanished tool list. A test that injects a cache init failure before the Service is created proves that the minimal MCP server starts, tools/list returns doctor, and calling doctor returns the startup-failure diagnostic with actionable text.
items.actor_trigger: MCP client starts gitcode-mcp --mcp server with cache path pointing to read-only directory, or schema-incompatible cache, or writer-locked cache, or injected cache init failure before Service creation
items.expected_outcome: tools/list always returns at least doctor tool; tool result or server capability includes typed diagnostic (cache_path_unwritable, schema_incompatible, cache_lock_contention, or startup-failure); calling doctor returns structured diagnostic with actionable text
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: MCP startup/readiness diagnostics with minimal MCP server fallback when cache/service init fails
items.target_surface: MCP server initialization and tools/list response over stdio transport

items.acceptance_criteria: A mocked issues sync test against a paginated provider with 50 records, a page size of 10, and a context cancelled before fetching page 4 proves: the sync stops before page 4, returns PartialSyncError with success_count = 30, and the diagnostic class is sync_cancelled. A test with --timeout 2s on a slow fixture proves the context deadline stops traversal and returns PartialSyncError with sync_timeout diagnostic class. A progress channel test proves that a consumer receives at least one progress event per page fetched. A mocked wiki sync test with a recursive tree provider and a context cancelled mid-traversal proves the internal tree walker stops within the current directory level and returns PartialSyncError with the records committed so far.
items.actor_trigger: User runs sync command or MCP sync_live tool with bounded page size and context cancelled before page 4 (issues) or mid-tree-traversal (wiki) or --timeout 2s on slow fixture
items.expected_outcome: Issues sync stops before page 4, returns PartialSyncError with success_count=30 and sync_cancelled diagnostic; --timeout triggers sync_timeout diagnostic; progress channel emits at least one event per page; wiki tree walker stops mid-level and returns PartialSyncError with committed records
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: Bounded collection sync with cancellation, progress, partial state, and internal wiki traversal changes
items.target_surface: CLI sync command and MCP sync_live tool over context-propagated HTTP traversal

items.acceptance_criteria: A mocked wiki sync test against a provider that returns 400 or 404 for GET contents of {repo}.wiki returns empty_wiki diagnostic class with actionable remediation text. A mocked create-page test against the same empty-wiki provider either successfully creates the wiki (proven by POST Home.md with base64 body + 201 + follow-up GET confirmation) or returns typed unsupported_wiki_uninitialized with a remediation path.
items.actor_trigger: User syncs or creates page against repo with uninitialized {repo}.wiki
items.expected_outcome: Sync returns empty_wiki diagnostic with actionable remediation text; create-page either bootstraps wiki via POST Home.md (201 + follow-up GET confirm) or returns unsupported_wiki_uninitialized with remediation path
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: Empty wiki bootstrap behavior with typed empty_wiki diagnostic and optional POST bootstrap
items.target_surface: CLI sync command and MCP sync_live tool against empty wiki; CLI create-page --live against empty wiki

items.acceptance_criteria: A mocked test syncs a wiki with remote page named Home.md; the cached record path field is wiki/Home.md. A mocked create-page test receives a provider response that returns HTTP 201 but lacks path and sha fields in the response body; the service either confirms success via a follow-up GET to contents/{path} returning the expected content and sha, or returns write_confirmation_incomplete diagnostic without caching the record as definitely written. A second test where the follow-up GET returns 200 with matching content proves the record is cached with http_attempted: true.
items.actor_trigger: User syncs wiki with Home.md remote page or creates wiki page against provider returning 201 without path/sha in body
items.expected_outcome: Cached record path is wiki/Home.md; create-page confirms via follow-up GET returning expected content+sha, or returns write_confirmation_incomplete; if follow-up GET confirms, record is cached with http_attempted=true
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: Initialized wiki path normalization (Home.md not Home.md.md) and create-page write confirmation decoding with follow-up GET verification
items.target_surface: Wiki sync cache record path and create-page write confirmation via POST /api/v5/repos/{repo}.wiki/contents/{path}

items.acceptance_criteria: A mocked test sets up a credential source that auth status reports as present. The same test runs a mocked add-comment --live command and successfully includes the bearer token in the outbound HTTP request. A second test where no credential is available proves that auth status reports credential_unavailable and create-issue --live fails with credential_unavailable before any outbound HTTP call. A third test with multiple credential sources proves the resolver picks the same source for both auth status and a write command.
items.actor_trigger: User runs auth status and then write command in same environment with env var GITCODE_TOKEN or multiple credential sources
items.expected_outcome: auth status reports credential present and add-comment --live includes bearer token; no-credential env fails both auth status and create-issue with credential_unavailable before HTTP call; multi-source env picks same source for both commands
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: Credential resolver parity: auth status, read probes, and write commands use same resolver pipeline
items.target_surface: CLI auth status, add-comment --live, create-issue --live commands with shared CredentialResolver

items.acceptance_criteria: A mocked issue create test with no labels in the request proves the outbound HTTP body does not contain the key labels. A mocked issue update test changing only the title proves no labels key in the serialized body. A mocked add-comment test returns HTTP 201 with live-shaped body; the service decodes it with http_attempted: true and caches the comment. A second test where the provider returns HTTP 201 but a malformed body proves the service reports http_attempted: true and schema_decode diagnostic class.
items.actor_trigger: User creates issue without labels, updates issue title only, or posts comment against provider returning live-shaped or malformed response
items.expected_outcome: Issue create/update bodies omit labels key; add-comment decodes live {id, note_id, body, created_at, user} response with http_attempted=true and caches comment; malformed body yields schema_decode diagnostic with http_attempted=true
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: Issue label omission (no labels key when unset) and add-comment response decoding with http_attempted and schema_decode classification
items.target_surface: Issue create/update POST payload serialization and add-comment POST /api/v5/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{num}/comments response decoding

items.acceptance_criteria: A mocked PR list test caches PR records of kind: pull_request with stable local source_id derived from number. A mocked PR detail test returns the individual record from cache. A mocked PR comments test caches comment records of kind: pr_comment linked to the parent PR. A mocked PR comment write test caches the comment with http_attempted: true. A test listing deployment-inhibited routes proves the adapter never calls pull_requests, merge_requests, or review_comments endpoints.
items.actor_trigger: User lists PRs, fetches PR detail, lists PR comments, or posts PR comment via live adapter
items.expected_outcome: PR list caches pull_request records with number-derived source_id; PR detail returns cached record; PR comments caches pr_comment records linked to parent PR; PR comment write receives 201 with {id, note_id, body} and caches with http_attempted=true; deployment-inhibited routes never called
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: PR read/comment route schema and cache design with kind: pull_request, kind: pr_comment, and deployment-inhibited route exclusion
items.target_surface: GitCode live adapter GET/POST /api/v5/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls and /pulls/{number}/comments routes with cache projection

items.acceptance_criteria: A mocked test simulates two concurrent read commands against the same cache path. Both commands complete without internal_error and return correct results. A test proves that a writer hold blocks other writers but does not block concurrent read commands when reads avoid the writer lock path. A test with three concurrent goroutines proves both readers complete while the writer is held, and the writer's diagnostic is typed cache_busy.
items.actor_trigger: Multiple concurrent read commands (search_sources) against same cache; writer hold during concurrent reads; three goroutines (two readers + one writer)
items.expected_outcome: Two concurrent reads complete without internal_error; writer contention yields cache_busy not internal_error; writer hold does not block readers; readers complete while writer is held; writer diagnostic is typed cache_busy
items.freshness: current binary build with mocked Go tests
items.request_item: Cache lock/concurrency diagnostic: typed cache_busy for lock contention, concurrent reads not blocked by writer, readers succeed while writer held
items.target_surface: Cache layer read/write paths under concurrent goroutine access
